entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 25 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-25 19:31PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Debris from China rocket launch to crash land - no one knows where
  • Woolworths and Pact commit to home brand packaging makeover
  • Evacuations, road closures in Sydney’s inner west after burst gas main
  • ASX set to rise despite Wall Street losses ahead of Fed meeting
  • Rules relaxed during COVID-19 pandemic set to stay and deliver $3.1b boost to economy
  • Are you in tune with your life season?
  • Medal fever: The events in which Australia could claim the trifecta at the Commonwealth Games
  • Trump 2.0 poses a grave risk to the US, and Australia
  • ‘Dalkeith of the Hills’: The ‘secret’ Perth suburb with no properties for sale
  • Scomo and Howard do a club lunch to reminisce on life after the Lodge
  • Ayres interviewed as part of independent inquiry into Barilaro trade appointment
  • The daughter of refugees, new MP Sally Sitou to reflect on parliament’s diversity
  • ‘I ask forgiveness’: Pope apologises for ‘deplorable evil’ of Canadian indigenous schools
  • ‘No deals’: Andrews government rules out pact with Greens in hung parliament
  • ABCC boss to keep $450,000 salary while building watchdog in limbo
  • You’re a walking urea factory. Could your pee help avert the fertiliser crisis?
  • Inclusivity has different meanings to many people
  • ‘Almost financially ruined us’: The number one complaint of new home buyers in NSW
  • Developers banned from locking apartment residents into energy deals
  • Redevelopment plans for historic Sydney rail workshops unveiled
  • Casuals, part-timers will get full paid domestic violence leave in new scheme
  • Government to rewrite climate bill to win over Greens
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Best of cartoons, July 26, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 25, 2022
  • A new prime minister, on eve of a new parliament, summons hope in silence
  • Morrison’s Tokyo trip fuels speculation about political future
  • Construction industry faces ‘raft of insolvencies’ without assistance
  • Kennedy signals new developments on AUKUS as she plans Solomons trip
  • From the Archives, 1972: Lone Tasman voyager lost
  • In the Herald: July 26, 1978
  • Mouthguards and the science of matter
  • NSW Building Commissioner David Chandler resigns
  • ‘Chaos’, confusion and 19 deaths: How COVID-19 took hold inside Newmarch House
  • Man guilty of advocating terrorism to be released from jail
  • Sydney councils in fierce battle over future home of SBS
  • ‘Porn king’ employee to be resentenced after some stealing convictions overturned
  • ‘I’m being intruded on and harassed’: Barilaro speaks after scuffle with cameraman
  • Thick fog and technical issue cause major delays at Sydney Airport
  • Britain to host 2023 Eurovision on Ukraine’s behalf
  • ‘Unprecedented, foolish, dangerous’: Keating attacks Pelosi’s planned trip to Taiwan
  • Jacinda Ardern says early signs that COVID-19 cases are falling
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • German chancellor and wife criticised after foxes scatter confidential papers
  • Myanmar junta executes democracy activists, the first such killings in decades
  • Robot breaks child’s finger during chess game
  • Russia tells famine-fearing Africa it’s not to blame for food shortage
  • On the campaign trail, many Republicans see a civil war
  • ‘Land banking’ by big developers driving up property prices: report
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Tuesday, July 26
  • Golfers to keep Northcote greens to themselves after stoush
  • Qld Speaker extends olive branch over changes to parliamentary scrutiny
  • Expansion of Brisbane’s domestic terminal shelved amid bigger plans
  • Qld police blamed disabled assault victim for ‘wanting to find a boyfriend’: inquiry
  • Bulk carrier crewman pulled from water off WA coast; search continues for missing fisherman
  • Parliamentary agenda must be balanced by budget restraint
  • EML Payments shares plunge as Irish central bank finds ‘shortcomings’
  • Airfares hit maximum altitude as capacity constraints hit the market
  • Crown’s Sydney casino set to open for gaming on August 8
  • COVID-19, supply chain disruptions bite miners as cost pressures rise
  • The US is setting up the RBA for some painful decisions
  • The Wrap: ASX falls flat after 20-day high amid tech slide
  • Travellers unfazed by COVID numbers as Flight Centre sees clear skies
  • Elon Musk accused of affair with Google co-founder’s wife
  • Mining activity in WA protected forests, reserves approved 33 times in 5 years
  • The NFT craze is coming to retailers near you
  • From Streeton to Whiteley: $9 million of art heading to auction
  • You think you know opera? Victorian Opera’s new AD will challenge you on that
  • ‘I prayed for an audition’: Aussie star Sam Reid follows in Tom Cruise’s footsteps
  • It’s bonkers and blasphemous, but there’s lots to like in Adam and Eve update
  • Stan Grant named as new sole host of Q+A
  • Margot, Delta, Guy and beyond: What to expect from the Neighbours finale
  • Twitter closing Australian office, staff to work from home
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Gazprom cuts gas flows to Europe; Russia says Odessa strike doesn’t violate grain deal
  • Pope apologizes for ‘evil committed by so many Christians’ in Canada’s residential schools
  • Deadly shootings in Whistler and Langley rock Canada’s British Columbia
  • Russia’s Gazprom to slash gas to Germany; Putin fosters uncertainty in Europe
  • Myanmar junta executes four pro-democracy activists
  • At least 17 Haitians dead after migrant boat capsizes off Bahamas
  • Zimbabwe goes for the gold — coin, that is — to fight high inflation
  • Tensions high between Israel, Russia over looming ban of Jewish Agency
  • U.K. to host 2023 Eurovision on behalf of Ukraine for ‘security’ reasons
  • Moldova ‘very worried’ Russia may invade, prime minister says
  • The law professor who set out to dismantle Tunisia’s democracy
  • Debris from China rocket launch to crash land — and no one knows where
  • In Okinawa, a push to revive a lost tattoo art for women, by women
  • The problem with ‘great power competition’
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia blames West for global food crisis despite blockade on Ukrainian grain
  • People protest growing insecurity in Congo's east
  • Police: 2 killed in shooting in Vancouver, B.C.
  • 'Earliest animal predator' named after David Attenborough
  • Pope apologizes for 'catastrophic' school abuses in Canada
  • Chess-playing robot breaks child’s finger at Moscow event
  • Mississippi public schools can make their own gun policies
  • Italy opens humanitarian corridor for 300 Afghan refugees
  • Bahamian officials search for survivors after boat sinks
  • UK parents lose life-support battle over 12-year-old son
  • Russian opposition figure detained after returning to Moscow
  • Hawaii has no girls in juvenile detention. Here’s how it got there.
  • Fire near Yosemite spreads into California’s largest of the season
  • Gunmaker’s Super Bowl stunt sheds light on marketing of ‘America’s rifle’
  • Woman gets prison time after her GoFundMe duped donors out of $400,000
  • Police sergeant charged after gripping colleague’s neck during arrest
  • This Republican embraced gun control. It ended his political career.
  • A worker paused to help find a missing girl. He found her waist-deep in a stream.
  • Post Politics Now: Biden’s covid symptoms ‘almost completely resolved,’ doctor says
  • Biden administration weighs declaring monkeypox a health emergency
  • Trump didn’t want to call for Jan. 6 rioters’ prosecution, new video shows
  • Jan. 6 committee could ‘contemplate a subpoena’ for Ginni Thomas
  • Stephen Buyer, former GOP congressman, accused of insider trading
  • What is a recession? Your economy questions, answered.
  • Boeing workers at 3 St. Louis-area factories vote to strike
  • The fight between authors and librarians tearing book lovers apart
  • Your boss might be reading your work messages. Here’s how to prevent that.
  • Path to home-flipping profit is not as simple as TV shows portray it
  • One-bedroom, one-bathroom Alexandria condo lists for $299,900
  • Trump Is Plotting to Blow Up the Constitution
  • Is Russia Letting Up on Energy? Don’t Trust Putin
  • Kissinger Knows Why the Global Leadership Deficit Is Getting Worse
  • Gen Z Thinks ‘The Money Will Come Back.’ Will It?
  • How and Why China Is Centralizing Its Billion-Ton Iron Ore Trade
  • How Italy’s Political Drama Forced an Election
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Elon Musk Sparked a Race to Send Satellites into ‘LEO’
  • I used to be optimistic about America’s future. Not anymore.
  • The Uvalde paradox: So many police, so little protection
  • The spotlight shifts in the clergy sex abuse scandal
  • Liz Cheney and Larry Hogan show what it means to stand against GOP lies
  • What do the Trumpists have planned? Turnkey authoritarianism.
  • Florida started penalizing bureaucratic delay. Housing permits spiked.
  • My video criticizing the Iranian government went viral. Then Instagram restricted my account.
  • Vladimir Kara-Murza from jail: I still intend to vote — and say no to the war
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • South Africa chasing 177 to beat England in third women’s cricket T20 international – live!
  • Sweden’s Asllani labels Euro VAR as catastrophic before England semi-final
  • Cristiano Ronaldo set for talks with Manchester United over future
  • Tory leadership: Former Conservative chair warns Liz Truss and Rishi Sunak not to ‘trash brand’ during debate – UK politics live
  • Pope Francis ‘begs forgiveness’ over abuse at church schools in Canada
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Moscow defends Odesa strikes; Gazprom further cuts gas supply to Germany – live
  • LIV golfers criticised by 9/11 survivors’ group over staging of New Jersey event
  • Woman on Florida boat gored by 100lb sailfish that jumped from water
  • Czech police turn seized Ferrari into patrol car
  • Met admits not sending Boris Johnson questionnaires over Partygate gatherings
  • Fossil of ‘earliest animal predator’ is named after David Attenborough
  • Canadian police say multiple homeless people shot in Vancouver suburb

  • Afghanistan is facing a climate calamity – it’s time the world took notice | Shadi Khan Saif

  • Reality Winner says she leaked file on Russia election hacking because ‘public was being lied to’

  • Would-be Tory leaders aren’t facing up to dire state of the NHS | Polly Toynbee

  • Did you solve it? Blockbusters!
  • Monkey attacks injure 42 people in Japanese city
  • There is one man to blame for the lorries backed up in Dover: Boris Johnson | Simon Jenkins
  • Take off your bra! Put on some weight! After decades of being talked down to, Kate Moss speaks out | Zoe Williams
  • Cruise passengers shuttled into Venice by motor boat to dodge big ships ban
  • Disney VIP world tour will produce 6.2 tonnes of carbon for each guest
  • Lower sex drive and hair loss among long Covid symptoms, finds study
  • Sixth man linked to 1989 Central Park rape case is exonerated
  • Ukraine grain exports set to start as Kyiv says US-supplied arms have slowed Russia
  • Wild salmon stocks at ‘crisis point’ with lowest on record in England

  • Freezing point climbs to record high above Swiss Alpine summits
  • Adele announces new dates for postponed Las Vegas residency
  • Putin’s attack on the grain deal was despicable. It also shows he’s desperate | Hamish de Bretton-Gordon
  • How we made Breakout by Swing Out Sister: ‘The record label hated it. It wasn’t cool to be upbeat’
  • Kent travel chaos: is there a fix and should Brexit take the blame? | Explainer
  • Two Americans killed while volunteering to defend Ukraine from Russia identified
  • ‘A brilliant guy’: tributes to holiday Briton feared drowned after rescuing his son
  • Monkeypox is truly an emergency. The WHO was right to raise the highest alarm | Devi Sridhar
  • Jaw-dropping news! Eating with your mouth open makes food taste better
  • Myanmar junta executes democracy activists in first such killings in decades
  • UK facing drought in August following extreme heat
  • Craters, queues, and goodbyes: a day in the life of a Ukrainian city
  • Thomasina Miers’ recipe for breaded haddock bun with roast chilli aïoli and watercress | The new flexitarian
  • The big idea: should we be using data to make life’s big decisions?
  • Premier League 2022-23 preview No 2: Aston Villa
  • The people making a difference: the former teacher saving the trees – and bats – threatened by HS2
  • How we met: ‘I found her on Facebook after 40 years and it felt like coming home’
  • Bob Rafelson: subtle and complex director was presiding genius of the Hollywood new wave
  • Euro 2022’s legacy must be true parity for female footballers in England | Marie-Christine Bouchier
  • The 20 best easy Mediterranean recipes
  • Amusan shatters 100m hurdles world record to revive super shoes debate
  • Is Murdoch tiring of Trump? Mogul’s print titles dump the ex-president
  • A new start after 60: ‘I didn’t want to be an invisible old lady – so I became a yoga teacher’
  • Plastic in the Depths: how pollution took over our oceans
  • ‘There’s never been a time when you could just say anything’: Frank Skinner on free speech, his bullying shame – and knob jokes
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 25 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-25 19:06PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong crime: deception cases increase as other major offences drop to lowest levels in 4 decades
  • Hong Kong lawmakers accuse education authorities of failure to boost patriotism in schools through more recognition of war memorial days
  • Fans of Hong Kong boy band Mirror out in droves for group’s concert, say real-name verification process ‘very smooth’
  • Developers’ shares rise on news of China’s US$12 billion bailout fund for beleaguered sector
  • Coronavirus: designated Hong Kong taxi drivers start taking travellers to quarantine hotels
  • Hong Kong’s anti-graft agency charges 2 former staff of duty-free shops company for allegedly inflating price of wine, reselling it for HK$26 million
  • Percentage of Hong Kong pupils getting secondary school place at 1 of their top 3 choices hits record high
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee gets policy address public consultation rolling, with meetings, district visits in works
  • Brother of late Hong Kong Canto-pop diva Anita Mui loses trademark lawsuit against biopic production company Edko Films
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong may shorten hotel quarantine and introduce colour codes for infected patients and arrivals around same time
  • Supporting Hong Kong students with special educational needs (SEN) – an estimated 57,000 children get help across local and international schools with the latter following voluntary guidelines
  • Hong Kong’s new environment minister pledges to move on green targets, start testing hydrogen powered cars by 2024
  • China wants Hong Kong to integrate with the Greater Bay Area, but can it convince young talent and employers it’s a good idea?
  • If Pelosi really wants peace to prevail in Taiwan, she’ll stay away
  • Has China’s population peaked? Drop in newborn screening underscores birth crisis
  • Thousands of jobseekers queue at Foxconn factory in Shenzhen, hoping for fat sign-on bonus as iPhone production season looms
  • China’s foreign policy to promote benevolent power while standing firm, Wang Yi says
  • Tech war: China’s ambassador meets with South Korean lawmaker amid Beijing’s growing concern over US-led chip alliance
  • China’s Wentian space lab docks with Tiangong station core module
  • Covid-19 in China: Shenzhen tightens controls amid BA.2 outbreak
  • NetEase quietly launches Diablo Immortal in China after month-long delay, but some players are disappointed
  • Wang Yi says South China Sea not a ‘fighting arena’ for great power games
  • Chinese manufacturers in Shenzhen, including Huawei and SMIC, go under ‘closed-loop’ operating mode amid Omicron outbreak
  • Chinese spending on belt and road projects remained low in first six months, report says
  • China detains woman over temple offerings for Japanese war criminals linked to Nanking massacre
  • China’s digital yuan tipped for wider application, while authorities down play privacy fears
  • Taiwan begins 5 days of war games simulating PLA attack
  • Coronavirus: China allows use of HIV drug Azvudine for adult Covid-19 patients
  • Chinese consumer e-commerce platform operator Eachnet.com to cease operations after 23 years amid tough market competition
  • China Mobile ends decade of failure against WeChat: iMessage-like Feixin to shut down after struggling in smartphone era
  • China steps up monkeypox procedures, calling on travellers to announce contact with cases
  • China-Africa relations: Beijing’s ambassador to Burkina Faso works to make up ground on delayed projects
  • China’s AI ambitions are being propelled by 75-year-old Harvard grad Andrew Chi-Chih Yao through impact on start-ups
  • Rishi Sunak vows to get tough on China, Britain’s ‘biggest long-term threat’
  • Chinese and US scientists build bridges with cutting-edge Hale telescope project
  • Philippines will be ‘a good neighbour’ but won’t yield territory, Marcos says in first policy speech
  • Japan confirms first monkeypox case
  • Taliban talks to Malaysia, Indonesia, other far-off Muslim-majority nations, ‘to create engagement perception’, say analysts
  • Coronavirus: North Korea touts ‘Koryo’ traditional medicine to fight Covid-19 as critics cast doubts
  • City in Japan takes on marauding macaques after 42 people hurt in spate of monkey attacks
  • Why are the Philippine heirs of a sultan clashing with Malaysia over Sabah?
  • Myanmar executes democracy activists in first use of capital punishment for decades
  • Solomon Islanders hope ‘China can care for us’ as opinions shift amid Beijing-backed building spree
  • What’s behind the rise of Japan’s Sanseito, a far-right party that loves Trump and hates immigration?
  • ‘Earliest animal predator’ named after British naturalist David Attenborough
  • Pope Francis apologises in Canada for Church’s role in ‘evil’ of residential indigenous schools
  • Gazprom to cut Nord Stream gas deliveries to Europe drastically from Wednesday
  • 33 dead in Kenya as bus plunges off bridge and into river
  • Canadian police report multiple shootings of homeless people in Vancouver suburb
  • Cabin crew member claims finding snake head in meal on SunExpress flight: report
  • Manko restaurant in Paris faces racism probe after denying entry to black women
  • Who is Nicole Shanahan, woman at centre of rift between Elon Musk and Sergey Brin?
  • Russia now seeking regime change in Ukraine, Lavrov says as Moscow expands war goals
  • Eyes on Donald Trump as he returns to Washington for first time since leaving White House
  • Russia charges dozens from Ukraine’s forces with ‘crimes against peace of humanity’
  • ‘Total BS’: Elon Musk denies affair with Google co-founder Sergey Brin’s wife
  • US semiconductor bill unites Bernie Sanders with conservative right in opposition to ‘corporate welfare’
  • 17 dead after boat carrying Haitian migrants sinks off Bahamas
  • Monkeypox ‘can be contained’ in US, White House doctor says after WHO triggers highest alert
  • Tesla’s Shanghai factory upgrade ‘boosts production by 30 per cent’ as the EV giant seeks to regain crown from China’s BYD
  • Hong Kong property firm K11 taps Paddington Bear, KidZania for 11 Skies complex’s first entertainment offerings
  • Hong Kong-listed firms from Tencent to SenseTime push stock buy-backs to new heights to defend valuations
  • Why Chinese buyers are still a force to reckon with in California’s property market
  • Goldman Sachs lowers MSCI China Index and earnings targets amid new headwinds for the Chinese economy
  • Hang Seng hits one-week low before Fed rate meeting while Goldman trims targets on Chinese stocks
  • AI, machine learning tech promises US$600 billion annually for China economy as it pervades industries, says McKinsey
  • Hong Kong braces for increase in prime interest rate after imminent US Fed action to rein in 40-year-high inflation
  • Buy Chinese developers? Mortgage boycott suggests stock rebound is built on fragile confidence
  • How to get a better night’s sleep – 7 lifestyle hacks, from a doctor who uses them in his daily life
  • Chinese food is being taken in exciting new directions in Europe and the US, and chefs are earning Michelin stars in the process
  • Caviar is on everything in Hong Kong, from dim sum to ice cream – but why is expensive ‘black gold’ so popular?
  • Why the Valentino of today bucks fashion stereotypes, according to creative director Pierpaolo Piccioli – ‘It’s not like the ivory towers of 50 years ago’
  • Trending mobile app BeReal gets users to post a photo in a 2-minute window and has soared in popularity in 2022, despite glitches
  • Pretty Heart movie review: Jennifer Yu anchors gentle film that’s part education drama, part romantic comedy and part family melodrama
  • From Japanese knives to utensils for sushi and ramen, this Tokyo neighbourhood is a treasure trove for chefs and tourists
  • Who is Pete Davidson’s little sister, Casey Davidson? Kim Kardashian and Kaia Gerber like her Instagram posts and Ariana Grande copied her tattoo, but she leads a low key life studying medicine
  • Where is Shania Twain now? A new Netflix documentary charts her rise to fame – these days she’s one of the richest singers in the world, with a net worth of US$400 million (despite a 15-year hiatus)
  • Who was Shonka Dukureh, Big Mama’s actress in Elvis? She starred in Baz Luhrmann’s biopic, performed with Doja Cat at Coachella and was about to release a blues album – but tragically died aged 44
  • Prince George turns 9! 9 things you didn’t know about Prince William and Kate Middleton’s son, from calling Queen Elizabeth ‘gan-gan’ to the British royal’s dreams of joining the RAF Air Cadets
  • Who is Tiggy Legge-Bourke, the vindicated ex-royal nanny? William and Harry’s former childminder won her lawsuit against the BBC, who lied to Princess Diana about an affair with Prince Charles
  • 7 Korean actors heading to Hollywood in 2022: Blackpink’s Jennie and HoYeon Jung will act with Lily-Rose Depp, Cha Eun-woo has a gig with Rebel Wilson, and Park Seo-joon is in Disney’s The Marvels
  • Haute Joaillerie Edit: Messika’s new high jewellery worthy of an Egyptian queen – the Parisian brand’s evocative Beyond the Light collection draws on everything from hieroglyphs to the Eye of Horus
  • Haute Joaillerie Edit: Chaumet’s latest Ondes et Merveilles high jewellery collection explores marine marvels, featuring weighty precious stones from diamonds to Colombia emeralds and pink tourmalines
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 25 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Brazil may export corn to China in the second half -minister
  • 'Goodfellas' actor Paul Sorvino dies at 83
  • West Ham's new recruit Aguerd undergoes ankle surgery
  • Pope Francis apologises for 'deplorable evil' of Canadian indigenous schools

  • Formula One teams want a quick decision on 2023 changes

  • Russia poses threat to Italian election, says centre-left chief

  • Fulham reach agreement to sign Israel forward Solomon

  • US Energy Department set to loan GM battery joint venture US$2.5 billion

  • Sri Lanka to restrict fuel imports for next 12 months
  • Pakistan's biggest city Karachi paralysed by monsoon rain

  • South Korean government, police clash on oversight
  • Mahathir received RM2.6 million political donation, says witness in Zahid’s corruption trial
  • Indonesia’s Jokowi leaves for visits to China, Japan and South Korea
  • Philippine President Marcos Jr vows no more COVID-19 lockdowns in his first State of the Nation address
  • China's Shenzhen orders big firms such as Foxconn into closed loop to curb COVID
  • Taiwan stages Chinese invasion war games with Ukraine in mind

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
